summary: Prepared R2 for the gameplay REQUEST_CHANGES with a narrow UI/i18n/test fix only. The blocked/unreachable-route decision assistant no longer names the disabled Review obligations control as the next action; it now explains that Review obligations is disabled there and tells the player to select a reachable district from the map or route queue first. The right-inspector Decision Data description no longer exposes raw/read-model implementation wording and instead describes population, labor, stores, obligations, supply route, governance, and campaign planning details. Existing grouped data, translated reason codes, and existing preview/confirmation callbacks remain unchanged.

decisions: Kept the fix presentation-only: the unreachable-route branch now uses new stable i18n keys for recommendation and next action, without adding command paths or UI rule authority.
Kept the Review obligations button disabled for unreachable routes; the assistant now explicitly explains that disabled state and names the existing map/route queue selection path first.
Replaced player-facing implementation jargon in the Decision Data description with gameplay terms tied to resources, obligations, supply route, governance, and campaign planning.
Added static web-shell and Playwright coverage for both R2 review points while preserving the existing reason-code translation and preview/confirmation tests.
risks: The R2 wording names the map and route queue selection path rather than introducing a new repair action; gameplay review should confirm that is enough guidance for blocked-route states.
No sim-core, protocol, save, content, Electron, manual node battle, or new command-surface changes were made.
requested_action: Review the two R2 fixes only: unreachable-route assistant guidance and removal of default player-facing read-model jargon. Do not route to QA until gameplay accepts R2.
